feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business Technology Platform
feed.subtitle: SAP Business Technology Platform Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Custom logback.xml Deployment on SAP BTP (tomee7)
entry.content:
I am currently deploying a Java application to the SAP BTP environment, using the following setup:

  • TARGET_RUNTIME: tomee7
  • Deployment Type: Java WAR application

My application includes a custom logback.xml configuration for logging purposes, which is placed under src/main/resources. However, upon deployment, I noticed that the application seems to use the default logback.xml provided by the tomee7 runtime, rather than the one included in my application package.

I would appreciate your support in clarifying the following:

  1. Is it possible to override the default logback.xml used by the tomee7 runtime with a custom configuration bundled in my WAR file?
  2. If yes, could you please advise:
    • The correct placement of the logback.xml file within the WAR structure?
    • Any additional configuration required to ensure the runtime uses my custom file?
  3. If not, is there a supported mechanism to achieve custom logging configurations within the tomee7 runtime on BTP?

entry.title: Could not create integration suite in my trail BTP account.
entry.content: I created the integration suit before but today it was not working properly, could not add a new flow. deleted it but could not add it again

entry.title: Integration flows do not work in the Integration Suite Trial version.
entry.content:
Starting from June 9, 2025, the issue began.
In the SAP Trial version, I am unable to create integration flows. I also cannot edit or deploy integration flows after importing them. Overall, none of the integration flow-related features are working.

entry.title: Integration flows do not work in the Integration Suite Trial version.
entry.content:
Starting from June 9, 2025, the issue began.
In the SAP Trial version, I am unable to create integration flows. I also cannot edit or deploy integration flows after importing them. Overall, none of the integration flow-related features are working.

1. Integration flow Selcet or Edit


2. Integration flow deploy

3. Integration Suite Version

entry.title: My BTP Trail account Subscription failed for Integration suite
entry.content:
Hello, I couldn't add my integration suite application in my BTP sub account's. As I have Subscription Failed error.

I need some here:

entry.title: How to Identify Your SAP BTP Global Administrator in SAP for Me
entry.content:
Symptom: You don't know who the BTP Global Admin is.

Requirements: You need to have an S-user and the role of Super Administrator or Cloud Administrator assigned in SAP for Me. 

  • If you don't have an S-User, please check this page.
  • If you are unsure which role you have, please check this note. 

Resolution:

1) Open SAP for Me and log in.

2) Navigate to the "Dashboards" section and click "Systems & Provisioning".

3) Select "Systems".

4) Search for "Business Technology Platform".

5) Click the link provided in the Global Account Column.

6) A new page will open. Scroll down to find the "Global Account Administrators" card.

Image 1

Image 2

Source: 2669325 - How to add a user as a SAP Business Technology Platform Global Account administrator

entry.title: SAP Service & Asset Manager - Reading data from local SSAM database in Dynamic Forms
entry.content:
Hello!

We have developed custom pages in MDK, in SSAM, to represent custom forms from our client within a work order operation. We are now evaluating if we can move these custom pages to SAP Dynamic Forms. We have not used this functionality before.

We have several ListPickers and conditions between fields that rely on reading information stored in the local database. For example, dynamic listPicker that shows current meters serial numbers synchronized into the app, with a dynamic query option.

We have seen in the product documentation, the functionality "Database Services" that somehow could work. However, it is advertised that the functionality is not currently supported.

Is it possible to do so with Dynamic Forms? Also, is it possible to pre populate fields in the form with data stored in the SSAM database?

thank you in advance for your help!!
Best regards,
Gonzalo.

entry.title: Blog Post Still 'Awaiting Review' After 24 Hours – Request for Update
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community Team,

I submitted a blog post titled "Build and Deploy a Full-Stack SAP CAPM App with Fiori UI on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry" more than 24 hours ago. It's still showing as “Awaiting Review”, and the URL is not publicly accessible yet.

Could you please let me know the estimated time for moderation or if any action is required from my side?

Thank you in advance for your support!

Best regards,
Madhan

entry.title: restore sap cloud connector connection Cloud To On-premise
entry.content:
Hello,

Can anyone tell me how to restore all the connections configured on the sap cloud connector (deleted by mistake): Cloud To On Premise.

Thanks & Best Regards

entry.title: Is there a BTP service or API to track user login/logout times and session duration?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm working on a project in SAP BTP and would like to know if there is any built-in service or API that can provide information such as:

  • When a user logged in

  • When they logged out

  • How long they stayed logged in (session duration)

This would help us monitor usage patterns and possibly audit access times.

Is there a service (like Audit Log, Identity Authentication, or another BTP capability) that offers this kind of data? If so, how can it be accessed?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation Subscription failed in BTP trail account
entry.content:
Hi,

I am getting the below failed message during Build process automation subscription. Please help on this?

Couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation. Try again. If the problem persists, please post a question to SAP Community.

Thanks.

entry.title: Issue with Destination Visibility in Cloud Identity Services – Connectivity Plan Required or Workaro
entry.content:
We are currently facing an issue when attempting to create a Source System in the Identity Provisioning Service. Specifically, the destination we've configured in our SAP BTP subaccount isn't appearing as an option in Cloud Identity Services. Based on our research, it seems we may need to create a Cloud Identity Services Connectivity Plan within the same subaccount, as suggested by this community thread: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/no-destination-available-at-identity-provisioning-service/qaq-p/12609309.

We have been following this comprehensive guide to enable Joule capabilities for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/joule-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-private-edition-a-comprehensive-setup-guide/ba-p/13786453.

However, we encountered an error while trying to create this Connectivity Plan, matching the scenario described in SAP Knowledge Base Article 3462424: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3462424.

Could the absence of this Connectivity Plan indeed be the root cause of our issue, or is there an alternative workaround available that would allow the already-created BTP destination to appear within the Cloud Identity Services? Any insights or guidance from the community would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Metric Dashboard availability in SAP Cloud Logging
entry.content:
Hi,

One of my customers wants to know when the Metric Dashboard that was available in the Application Logging Service (which provides visibility into CPU and RAM utilization for each application instance) will be part of the Cloud Logging Service.

Any timelines or updates on this ?

Thanks,

entry.title: Test SAPUI5 app locally with multiple deployed CAPM services each with its own XSUAA instance.
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,
I have a SAPUI5 app that consumes 2 or more CAPM services, and each service has its own bound xsuaa instance. I am using @SAP/approuter to test the UI5 app locally with deployed services. I tried adding the two xsuaa credential details into the xsuaa array inside the default-env.json. Still, when I run the local approuter using npm start, I get an error that says 'No UAA service found' as seen in the screenshot below.
For now, I have to deploy the app and test in BTP launchpad every time. How can I achieve local testing with multiple xsuaa instances? 

File: default-env.json - PS: xsuaa json objects are collapsed due to sensitive info.

local-test-approuter / package.json - I have the latest @SAP/approuter

local-test-approuter / xs-app.json

Any suggestions and solutions will be greatly appreciated.

Thanks & Cheers,
Naresh

entry.title: Joule service in SAP BTP
entry.content:
I would like to add Joule service in my BTP cockpit as shown in the image below.  However, I couldn't find the Joule service in the Entitlements section.

Could you please advise how to add the Joule service and what prerequisites are required to be able to assign it?

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

entry.title: landscape portal not visible even after subscribing booster in SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi All ,

We have subscribed to SAP BTP ABAP environment but it is not started as it seems to be hibernated .  SAP suggests to use landscape portal . To start it we need Landscape portal service. We have added it through booster and completed the configuration . But its not visible anywhere. As per the steps 3 subaccounts were supposed to be created but we cannot see anything.

Region selected was US-east when completing the steps,

Please let me know what is missing here . Should we add the entitlement manually and do we need to select subaccount in region europe?

Please suggest . We are stuck and we cannot access SAP btp fiori launchpad from ABAP environment as its hibernated and we need to log in landscape portal to start it.

Regards

Sweta

entry.title: Issue with setting up connectivity from BTP to Converged Cloud
entry.content:
We are trying to run a script as a Cloud Foundry function on BTP to pull data from Converged Cloud (asset data) , and we are failing to connect despite having the cloud connector set up (On the cloud connector page, it says "available" for the converged cloud endpoints that we set up). Also, the code runs locally just fine.

Did someone else face a similar challenge, or do you have any idea who we could work with to troubleshoot this?

Thanks in advance for your time and help!

Petr

The error:

Authentication error at https://identity-3.ap-ae-1.cloud.sap/v3/auth/tokens: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='identity-3.ap-ae-1.cloud.sap', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /v3/auth/tokens (Caused by NameResolutionError("<urllib3.connection.HTTPSConnection object at 0x10689f0e0>: Failed to resolve 'identity-3.ap-ae-1.cloud.sap' ([Errno 8] nodename nor servname provided, or not known)"))

entry.title: How to Implement Dynamic XSLT or Groovy Transformations in SAP CPI Based on Message Context?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I'm working on a complex integration scenario using SAP Cloud Integration (CPI), where incoming messages can vary in structure and must be transformed differently depending on:

  • The message content (specific fields or data structure)

  • Custom headers or properties passed from upstream systems

I want to apply different XSLT or Groovy scripts at runtime, without creating multiple iFlows or hardcoding every possible condition into a single transformation.

My Questions:

  1. How can I dynamically apply different XSLT or Groovy scripts based on runtime conditions (e.g., a header field like Document Type)?

  2. Is there a way to modularize and externalize scripts or transformations so they can be reused or loaded conditionally in the iFlow?

  3. What are the performance considerations or limitations when using conditional script execution or dynamic resource loading in CPI?

  4. Can this be combined with Content Modifier or Router patterns to maintain readability and reusability of the iFlow?

  5. Are there any recommended design patterns for managing complex transformation logic dynamically in CPI projects?

What I’ve Tried:

  • I’ve used Groovy scripts with if-else blocks to call different logic branches, but this becomes hard to manage at scale.

  • I’ve explored externalizing scripts using Script Collection, but couldn’t find a way to dynamically load them at runtime based on message properties.

  • I read that dynamic script execution might be possible with evaluate() in Groovy, but I’m unsure of its security and performance impact in CPI.

Goal:

Design a scalable and maintainable approach to apply runtime-dependent transformations (XSLT or Groovy) within a single iFlow based on message metadata or structure.

Any best practices, examples, or gotchas would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance,
YERUVA PRANEETH REDDY

entry.title: Not able to create a new account of BTP trial
entry.content:
Dear Team,

While trying to create a new BTP trial account, it is not creating one, with the message as 'Due to high demand, signing to a new account is currently not possible.'

Can you please support.

Regards.

entry.title: S/4 Hana Extensibility Service Instance
entry.content:
I have an application on SAP BTP. The application has few service bindings. For example : application-x has eleven bindings. Ten of them are of type s4-hana-cloud and one of them is xsuaa. I do understand that why we have binded xsuaa but I don't understand the use case of s4-hana-cloud bindings. 

Why do we need instance of s4-hana-cloud ? Also, I want to learn side by side extension development. Is there a good tutorial that outlines step by step guide? I want to use Cloud SDK for typescript code generation and then call some API from SAP Business Acclerator Hub. I understand few things. For example : Downloading edmx, then use cloud SDK to generate code and use that code on my service, creating destination. But I don't know how to put all these together. 

Help me with this. Thanks. Any paid course or resource is also highly appreciated. If you have question , then don't hesitate to ask.
